obligations.reason: Debug JTAG path may bypass authentication for DMI bus access. The presence of a jtag_key input and a DMI bridge requires checking that all DMI transactions (reads, writes, and SBA accesses) are gated by a successful key comparison before reaching the hart or system bus.
obligations.subject: external debugger via JTAG/DMI
obligations.operation: DMI read/write and system bus access (SBA)
obligations.object: hart debug CSRs, system bus memory/peripherals
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_key == expected secret value; debug authentication state latched after correct key presentation
obligations.candidate_path: dmi_jtag -> dm_top -> dm_csrs -> hart, and dmi_jtag -> dm_top -> dm_sba -> system bus
state_conditions_to_check: jtag_key input value and comparison result
dmactive bit state
dmi_access flow control (shift_dr, update_dr)
reset state of authentication latch
required_evidence_slots: RTL implementation of jtag_key comparison in dmi_jtag
Gating logic for DMI request valid into dm_top
Enable condition for debug_req_o and SBA commands
knowledge_used: Lock/Sticky Bit/Write Protection for debug enable
obligations.uncertainty: Whether jtag_key is actually used as an authentication token inside dmi_jtag, and whether the comparison is robust against timing/replay attacks.

obligations.reason: ROM2 stores a key (pkey_loc_o) that feeds jtag_key. If ROM2 is writable from any bus master, the key can be replaced, defeating debug authentication.
obligations.subject: any bus master (CPU, debug SBA)
obligations.expected_guard: ROM2 write port is permanently disabled (we_i tied low) or address decode restricts writes to authorized state.
obligations.candidate_path: Bus master -> system bus -> ariane_peripherals -> rom2 interface (we_i, wdata_i)
state_conditions_to_check: ROM2 we_i signal generation in ariane_peripherals
Address mapping for ROM2 in system bus fabric
Reset state of ROM2 write protection logic (if any)
required_evidence_slots: rom2.sv module internals showing how we_i is handled (e.g., write ignored or disabled)
ariane_peripherals connection of rom2_we signal
Any write-protection lock that could be bypassed
knowledge_used: Crypto/Secret State Exposure via writable memory
obligations.uncertainty: rom2.sv is a small module; need to confirm if we_i is internally ignored or if writability is allowed under certain modes.

obligations.reason: Machine-mode CSR registers (mstatus, medeleg, mideleg, etc.) must not be writable from lower privilege modes (U-mode, S-mode). The csr_regfile module contains privilege checks; an error could elevate privileges.
obligations.subject: hart CPU in user/supervisor mode
obligations.operation: CSR write instruction to machine-mode registers
obligations.expected_guard: Privilege mode check in csr_regfile: only M-mode or debug mode can write machine-mode CSRs; S/U modes raise illegal instruction exception.
obligations.candidate_path: Instruction execution -> CSR write request -> csr_regfile address decode and privilege validation
state_conditions_to_check: Current privilege level (umode_i, debug_mode_q)
CSR address (csr_addr_i) and read_access_exception generation logic
write enable (csr_we) condition
required_evidence_slots: Privilege check implementation for each CSR write case
Handling of read_access_exception for write attempts
obligations.uncertainty: Whether debug mode overrides all privilege checks and whether that could be exploited if debug access is unauthenticated.

obligations.reason: Debug mode may grant unrestricted CSR access. If debug authentication is bypassed (see OBL-001), an attacker could modify security-critical CSRs such as debug_mode itself, PMP, or translation control registers.
obligations.subject: debug module (via DMI) once authenticated
obligations.operation: write to protected CSRs while hart is in debug mode
obligations.object: security-critical CSRs: debug_mode, pmpcfg, satp, mstatus.MPRV, etc.
obligations.expected_guard: Debug mode writes must still respect certain lock bits or require extra authorization; or formal specification of debug CSR accessibility.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI -> dm_top -> dm_csrs -> hart debug mode -> CSR write path
state_conditions_to_check: debug_mode_q state
Whether debug mode bypasses all CSR write protections
Any per-CSR write-protection bits unaffected by debug mode
required_evidence_slots: CSR write logic in debug mode inside csr_regfile
List of registers that are writable in debug mode
Documentation/code comments regarding debug CSR access
obligations.uncertainty: RISC-V debug spec allows debug mode to access most CSRs; the vulnerability depends on whether debug mode itself can be entered without authorization.

obligations.reason: Testmode inputs (testmode_i) are present in dm_csrs, dm_top, dmi_jtag, dmi_jtag_tap. These could enable scan chains or bypass authentication, leaking the jtag_key or enabling debug without authentication.
obligations.subject: test/scan controller via testmode_i signal
obligations.operation: scan out registers, bypass JTAG key comparison
obligations.object: jtag_key register, authentication state, internal CSRs
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i is de-asserted or isolated from security-sensitive logic; scan chains do not include key material.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i -> internal logic in dmi_jtag, dm_top, dm_csrs, dmi_jtag_tap
state_conditions_to_check: How testmode_i affects dmi_access, shift_dr, update_dr
Whether testmode can force debug enable or bypass reset
required_evidence_slots: Definition of testmode_i signal route in each module
Any security-sensitive logic gated by testmode_i
obligations.uncertainty: Need to verify actual use of testmode_i; it might be unused or tied low in integration.

obligations.reason: DMI reset signals (dmi_reset, dmihardreset) may clear authentication state, allowing an attacker to reset the debug module and then use debug without re-authentication.
obligations.subject: external JTAG controlling DMI reset sequences
obligations.object: debug authentication latch (key comparison result), dmactive bit
obligations.expected_guard: DMI reset does not clear authentication latch, or after reset, debug remains disabled until key is presented again.
obligations.candidate_path: dmi_jtag_tap dmi_reset_o -> dm_csrs/dmi_jtag -> internal debug state
state_conditions_to_check: Effect of dmi_reset on key_valid or similar authentication signal
dmactive reset value
dmihardreset vs dmireset differences
required_evidence_slots: Internal signal for key_valid or jtag_key comparison outcome in dmi_jtag
Effect of reset lines on that signal
obligations.uncertainty: Whether authentication is implemented as a sticky bit that survives reset or a volatile comparison that resets.

obligations.reason: The system bus access (SBA) feature in the debug module can read/write arbitrary memory. If SBA is enabled without authentication, it can read ROM2 (jtag_key source) or write to memory-protected regions (e.g., secure monitor memory).
obligations.subject: debugger via SBA
obligations.operation: memory read/write to ROM2 address or other sensitive regions
obligations.object: ROM2 key storage, secure memory regions
obligations.expected_guard: SBA transactions are gated by the same authentication as DMI, or address decode prevents SBA from accessing ROM2 range.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_csrs sbaddress/sbdata -> dm_sba -> system bus fabric -> ariane_peripherals -> rom2
state_conditions_to_check: SBA enable condition (dmactive, authentication state)
Address routing in system bus; whether ROM2 is accessible to SBA master
Whether ROM2 read address space overlaps with SBA accessible region
required_evidence_slots: SBA master enable logic in dm_top/dm_csrs
System address map for ROM2 vs debug master visibility
Any PMP-like protection on SBA accesses
knowledge_used: Crypto/Secret State Exposure via debug DMA
obligations.uncertainty: The exact address mapping and whether the SBA master is subject to any PMP checks needs verification.

obligations.reason: The debug request signal (debug_req_o) can halt the hart and enter debug mode. If debug_req is asserted without authentication, an attacker can stop the processor and potentially exploit debug mode.
obligations.subject: external debugger
obligations.expected_guard: debug_req_o is forced inactive until successful jtag_key authentication.
obligations.candidate_path: dmi_jtag -> dm_top -> dm_csrs -> hartsel, haltreq generation
state_conditions_to_check: Gating of debug_req_o by key_valid or authentication state within dm_top/dm_csrs
Whether dmactive can be set without key
required_evidence_slots: Logic that generates debug_req_o from DMI commands
The dependency (if any) on a key authentication signal
obligations.uncertainty: Whether the RISC-V debug spec allows unauthenticated halting; the product may consider that acceptable, but for a security review it is a vulnerability.

obligations.reason: The dmactive bit (debug module active) might be set to enable debug and later cleared to disable it. If there is no sticky lock to permanently disable debug after a secure boot phase, an attacker could re-enable debug later.
obligations.subject: external debugger
obligations.operation: write dmactive=1 via DMI after it was cleared
obligations.expected_guard: A sticky lock bit (e.g., dmactive_lock) prevents dmactive from being set again after it was cleared, until a system reset.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI write to dmcontrol register (dmactive field) in dm_csrs
state_conditions_to_check: Existence of a dmactive_lock or similar lock bit
Reset value of dmactive and any lock
Whether dmactive can be cleared by software (hart) and then later set by debugger
required_evidence_slots: DM control register implementation in dm_csrs for dmactive bit
Any logic that latches a permanent lock state
obligations.uncertainty: The design may not implement such a lock; it may be a missing security feature rather than a direct bug.

obligations.reason: The jtag_key signal is an input to dmi_jtag. It may be observable through JTAG boundary scan or internal test/debug features if not explicitly excluded.
obligations.subject: scan chains or debug observability logic
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_key is excluded from scan chains; test capture does not include it; or testmode_i is disabled in production.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i -> dmi_jtag/dmi_jtag_tap internals, boundary scan cells
state_conditions_to_check: Whether jtag_key is part of any shift register or data signal observable during capture_dr
Whether dtmcs_select or bypass paths expose key bits
required_evidence_slots: RTL code showing where jtag_key is registered or used in dmi_jtag
Any shift register path that includes that register
Testmode influence on key comparison logic
obligations.uncertainty: The jtag_key might be compared asynchronously and not stored, reducing the exposure window.

obligations.reason: Performance counters in the RISC-V core can potentially leak information about executed code (side channel). If accessible from user mode, they may violate a security policy.
obligations.subject: user-mode software
obligations.operation: read performance counters via CSR instructions
obligations.expected_guard: mcounteren CSR bit settings restrict access to M/S modes only, or counters are disabled for user-mode reads.
obligations.candidate_path: CSR read address decode -> performance counter read path -> csr_rdata_o
state_conditions_to_check: mcounteren register value
read_access_exception generation for user-mode counter reads
Counter enables tied to privilege mode
required_evidence_slots: CSR read access logic for performance counters in csr_regfile
Implementation of mcounteren and scounteren
obligations.uncertainty: Standard RISC-V behavior allows M-mode to configure counter accessibility; if the hardware correctly implements the specification, this is not a bug but a configuration choice.

evidence_slots.subject: external debugger via JTAG/DMI
evidence_slots.operation: DMI read/write and system bus access (SBA)
evidence_slots.object: hart debug CSRs, system bus memory/peripherals
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMI transactions gated by successful jtag_key comparison
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: No key comparison is performed; jtag_key is stored in a `pass` register but never compared against any expected value. DMI requests are unconditionally forwarded to dm_top/dm_csrs.
evidence_slots.path: dmi_jtag.sv: `pass` register assigned on rst_ni, but no usage in enable logic. dmi_req_valid_o is generated solely from DMI FSM state, no gating by `pass_chk` or similar. In dm_csrs.sv line 171, `dmstatus.authenticated` is hardwired to 1'b1.
evidence_slots.state_condition: jtag_key present but unused; dmactive set via DMI; no authentication latch.
evidence_slots.impact: Attacker can enable full debug (halt, CSR access, SBA memory read/write) without knowing the key. This defeats the entire debug authentication intent.

rtl_evidence.comment: pass <= jtag_key on reset, but no comparison logic in always_comb/always_ff steering writes/reads
dmi_req_valid is 1'b0 default and set for Idle->Read/Write transitions, independent of key
dmstatus.authenticated = 1'b1; authentication always reported as passed

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Complete absence of debug authentication enforcement.
inspection_records.notes: This is the root cause for many downstream violations (OBL-007,008,009).

evidence_slots.subject: any bus master (CPU, debug SBA)
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM2 write port permanently disabled or address decode restricts writes
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rom2.sv uses `we_i` to conditionally write `wdata_i` into `secure_reg`. In ariane_peripherals.sv, `rom2_we` is connected to an AXI-to-memory adapter that passes bus write commands. No write protection exists.
evidence_slots.path: AXI bus -> ariane_peripherals i_axi2rom2 -> rom2_we -> rom2.sv we_i -> `secure_reg[addr] <= wdata_i`
evidence_slots.state_condition: req_i high and we_i high during a write transaction.
evidence_slots.impact: An attacker (including debugger via SBA) can overwrite the jtag_key and other secret keys stored in ROM2, breaking the only credential for debug access.

rtl_evidence.comment: if(req_i) if(!we_i) read else write secure_reg
rom2_we driven from bus write, not tied to 0

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Secret key storage is writable without restriction.

evidence_slots.subject: hart CPU in user/supervisor mode
evidence_slots.operation: CSR write instruction to machine-mode registers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Privilege mode check raises illegal instruction for S/U writes to M-mode CSRs
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: csr_regfile.sv implements privilege check: `if ((riscv::priv_lvl_t'(priv_lvl_o & csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) != csr_addr.csr_decode.priv_lvl) && !(csr_addr.address==riscv::CSR_MEPC))` raises illegal instruction. Machine-mode CSRs have priv_lvl M, so S/U writes are blocked.
evidence_slots.state_condition: csr_we or csr_read true, priv_lvl_o does not match required level
evidence_slots.impact: No privilege escalation via CSR writes from lower modes.

rtl_evidence.comment: privilege check logic
read_access_exception and update_access_exception used for illegal instruction

inspection_records.notes: Standard RISC-V privilege enforcement appears correct.

evidence_slots.subject: debug module (via DMI) once authenticated
evidence_slots.operation: write to protected CSRs while hart is in debug mode
evidence_slots.object: security-critical CSRs: debug_mode, pmpcfg, satp, mstatus.MPRV, etc.
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Debug mode writes respect lock bits or require extra authorization
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In csr_regfile.sv, `priv_lvl_o` is forced to `PRIV_LVL_M` when `debug_mode_q` is high (line 938). The privilege check uses `priv_lvl_o`, so all machine-mode CSRs become writable. No additional locks are checked.
evidence_slots.path: debug_mode_q -> priv_lvl_o = M; then CSR write privilege check passes for any M-mode CSR
evidence_slots.impact: If debug mode is entered (via unauthenticated DMI), attacker can modify PMP, satp, etc., gaining full system control.

rtl_evidence.comment: assign priv_lvl_o = (debug_mode_q || umode_i) ? riscv::PRIV_LVL_M : priv_lvl_q;
privilege check passes for M-mode CSRs when priv_lvl_o is M

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Debug mode unconditionally elevates privilege to M-mode, granting write access to all M-mode CSRs without separate checks.
inspection_records.notes: Consequence of OBL-001 if not fixed.

evidence_slots.subject: test/scan controller via testmode_i signal
evidence_slots.operation: scan out registers, bypass JTAG key comparison
evidence_slots.object: jtag_key register, authentication state, internal CSRs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: testmode_i de-asserted or isolated from sensitive logic; scan chains exclude key material
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: In dmi_jtag_tap.sv, testmode_i selects between inverted and non-inverted TCK via `pulp_clock_mux2`. It does not affect state machines or enable bypass. In other modules, testmode_i is passed through but not observed to gate authentication.
evidence_slots.state_condition: testmode_i = 1 selects bypass clock, no security state change
evidence_slots.impact: No direct security bypass observed, but full scan chain integration not visible.

rtl_evidence.comment: testmode_i used as clk_sel_i for clock inverter mux
testmode_i passed to i_dmi_jtag_tap, not used locally

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Missing visibility into full DFT scan insertion and whether key register is excluded.
inspection_records.notes: If synthesis/DFT inserts scan on key register, testmode could expose it, but this is not evident from RTL.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG controlling DMI reset sequences
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMI reset does not clear authentication
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: No authentication latch exists (see OBL-001). The `pass` register holding jtag_key is reset only by `rst_ni` (line 230), not by `dmi_reset` or `trst_ni`. `dmactive` is cleared by synchronous reset in dm_csrs.sv but can be re-set.
evidence_slots.path: dmi_reset_o from tap does not affect `pass` register
evidence_slots.impact: Not applicable as there is no authentication to reset.

rtl_evidence.comment: pass is only loaded on rst_ni (system reset), not trst_ni or dmi_reset
dmactive cleared and re-writable, no sticky lock

inspection_records.notes: No authentication present; reset resilience is irrelevant.

evidence_slots.subject: debugger via SBA
evidence_slots.operation: memory read/write to ROM2 address or other sensitive regions
evidence_slots.object: ROM2 key storage, secure memory regions
evidence_slots.expected_guard: SBA transactions gated by authentication or address protection
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs.sv implements SBA control registers (SBCS, SBAddress, SBData). Writing SBAddress via DMI sets sbaddress_o, and the dm_sba module issues AXI reads/writes. No authentication signal gates these operations. The AXI master is visible to the system bus, and ROM2 is addressable in the peripheral region.
evidence_slots.path: DMI write to SBAddress -> dm_csrs sbaddress_o -> dm_sba -> AXI bus -> ariane_peripherals rom2 interface
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmactive=1, DMI access allowed, SBAddress written
evidence_slots.impact: With OBL-001 (no authentication), attacker can read the jtag_key from ROM2 via SBA, or write to it.

rtl_evidence.comment: SBAddress0/1 write enables sbaddress_write_valid_o without authentication check
dm_sba integration, no key gating
rom2 accessible via AXI fabric

inspection_records.candidate_finding: SBA bus master is unauthenticated and can access secret key storage.

evidence_slots.subject: external debugger
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dm_csrs.sv allows writing dmcontrol.haltreq via DMI, which drives haltreq_o. dm_top wires haltreq to dm_mem, which generates debug_req_o. No authentication signal inhibits this path.
evidence_slots.path: DMI write to DMControl -> dmcontrol_q.haltreq -> haltreq_o -> dm_top hartsel/haltreq -> debug_req_o
evidence_slots.impact: Attacker can halt the hart without key, enabling subsequent debug mode entry.

rtl_evidence.comment: writing dmcontrol sets haltreq_o immediately if dmactive
haltreq connected to debug_req_o generation

evidence_slots.subject: external debugger
evidence_slots.operation: write dmactive=1 via DMI after it was cleared
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmcontrol_q.dmactive is a single writable bit. There is no dmactive_lock or any persistent lock. Writing 1 to dmactive via DMI sets it. After a clear, it can be set again.
evidence_slots.path: DMI write to DMControl -> dmcontrol_d.dmactive -> dmcontrol_q.dmactive
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmactive bit cleared voluntarily; no lock bit exists
evidence_slots.impact: A secure boot sequence that disables debug can be undone by an attacker re-enabling debug.

rtl_evidence.comment: dmactive is part of dmcontrol register, written via dmi_req_i.data
when dmactive=0, most fields cleared but dmactive itself can be rewritten

inspection_records.candidate_finding: No sticky lock on debug module activation after deactivation.
inspection_records.notes: This is a missing feature rather than a bug in existing logic.

evidence_slots.subject: scan chains or debug observability logic
evidence_slots.expected_guard: jtag_key excluded from scan chains; test capture does not include it
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: The `pass` register holding jtag_key is not part of any shift register path. During capture_dr, only `{address_q, data_q, error_q}` are loaded into dr_d. testmode_i only selects clock, no access to `pass`. No other path exposes the key.
evidence_slots.path: capture_dr loads dr_d from address/data/error, not pass
evidence_slots.state_condition: capture_dr=1 in dmi_jtag, dr_d assignment excludes pass
evidence_slots.impact: JTAG scan cannot directly extract the stored key.

rtl_evidence.comment: dr_d assignment in capture_dr includes address_q, data_q, error_q, no pass
pass register stored separately, not in shift path

inspection_records.notes: Key is not observable through defined JTAG debug paths.

evidence_slots.subject: user-mode software
evidence_slots.operation: read performance counters via CSR instructions
evidence_slots.expected_guard: mcounteren CSR bits restrict access or counters disabled for U-mode
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: CSR read process does not check mcounteren; reading CSR_MCYCLE, CSR_MINSTRET returns value without privilege restriction. The privilege check passes because these CSRs have U-mode privilege level (implicitly). mcounteren is hardwired to 64'b0, and scounteren also 64'b0, but that means counteren bits are all zero, which should disable user-mode access. However, the hardware does not enforce this; reads still succeed because the privilege check only ensures priv_lvl_o >= priv_lvl (which is U, so always passes).
evidence_slots.path: csr_read_process -> unique case -> CSR_MCYCLE: csr_rdata = cycle_q; no counteren check
evidence_slots.state_condition: csr_read=1, csr_addr.address = MCYCLE/MINSTRET, any privilege level
evidence_slots.impact: User-mode can read cycle/instret counters, potentially enabling side-channel attacks.

rtl_evidence.comment: CSR_MCYCLE, CSR_MINSTRET read directly without checking mcounteren
M/S counteren reads return 64'b0, indicating no enable logic

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Performance counters are accessible to user mode without counteren enforcement.
inspection_records.notes: Could be a configuration issue; the hardware should block reads when counteren bits are zero.
